content:
# Berera Formation

**Summary**: A sandstone formation used as a comparison for acidizing treatment effectiveness.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2016-hamza|HAMZA, 2016]] used Berea samples to evaluate the solubility of different chelating agents.

- [[2016-jad|Jad, 2016]] investigated the damage of Berea sandstone core samples.

## Depositional Environment



- [[2016-jad|Jad, 2016]] investigated the damage of Berea sandstone core samples.

## Reservoir Properties

- [[2016-hamza|HAMZA, 2016]] Porosity 17-18.85% (based on similar samples).

## Diagenetic History



## Key Findings Across Sources

- Berea rock showed lower solubility ratios compared to Bandera across all tested chelating agents. ([[2016-hamza|HAMZA, 2016, p. 61]])

- Berea sample BE3 showed a shift in T2 distribution after deionized water injection, indicating pore shrinkage. ([[2016-jad|Jad, 2016, p. 89]])
